# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKV } from "https://deno.land/x/kv@v0.5.2/mod.ts";

const kv = await openKV({ namespace: "edge" });

export async function getEdgeDbApi(req: any) {
  const queryParam = req.params.query;
  if (!queryParam) return { status: 400, body: { error: "Missing query parameter" } };

  try {
    const params = JSON.parse(queryParam);
    for (const param in params) {
      if (params[param] === "" || params[param] === null) {
        delete params[param];
      }
    }

    let filterParam = "";
    for (const param in params) {
      filterParam += `${param} == "${params[param]}" and `;
    }
    filterParam = filterParam.slice(0, -5);

    const keys = ["users"];
    for (const user of req.query.users || []) {
      keys.push(["users", user]);
    }
    keys.push(["_search", filterParam]);

    const results = await Promise.all(
      keys.map(async (key) => {
        return { key, value: await kv.get(key.join("/")) };
      })
    );

    if (!results.some((result) => result.value !== null)) {
      return { status: 404, body: "Not Found" };
    }

    const responses = results
      .filter((result) => result.value !== null)
      .map((result) => ({ key: result.key, value: JSON.parse(result.value || "{}") }));

    if (results.length > 0) {
      for (const response of responses) {
        const user = response.value;
        if (!user.deleted && !req.query.users.includes(user.userId)) {
          await kv.put(`${response.key.join("/")}`, JSON.stringify({ ...user, deleted: true }));
        }
      }
    }

    return { status: 200, body: responses.map((result) => result.value) };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return { status: 500, body: "Internal Server Error" };
  }
}
